title: carya ovata f. nuttallii
intro: Carya ovata f. nuttallii, also known as the Shagbark Hickory, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The Shagbark Hickory tree is valued for its unique bark and edible nuts, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The nuts and bark of the Shagbark Hickory contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and improve overall health. Shagbark Hickory is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been used to support digestive health, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
